sutta: once when the exalted 1 was staying in the brick hall and nadika he addressed the monk saying monks lord they replied and the exalted 1 said monks mindfulness of death when developed made to increase is very fruitful great in real merging in the deathless having the deathless as consummation monks developed mindfulness of death more and when he had spoken thus among said to him i lord develop mindfulness of death how so monk hearing lord such is my thought were i day and night to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word much would be done by me thus lord i developed mindfulness of death and another said i too lord develop mindfulness of death how so among hearing lord such is my thought will i day long to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word much would be done by me and another said such is my thought will i to abide mindful just so long as i eat an arms meal and another said why to abide mindful as i munch and 4 or 5 morsels of food and another said as i munch and swallow only 1 morsel of food and another said i to lord develop mindfulness of death how so monk lord such is my thought for i to abide in just so long as i breathe in and out or breathe out and in mindful of the exalted 1's word much would be done by me thus lord i develop mindfulness of death and when he had spoken thus the exalted 1 said to the monks monks the monk who makes mindfulness of death who develops mindfulness of death thus will i day and night to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word or he who thinks thus will i day long to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word or the 1 who thinks as i eat an arms meal or as i munch and swallow 4 or 5 morsels of food mindful of the exalted words much would be done by me those monks are said to live indolently slightly they develop mindfulness of death for the destruction of the asavas but the monk who makes mindfulness who develops mindfulness of death thus will i to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word as i munch and swallow 1 morsel of food and he who thinks thus for i to abide mindful of the exalted 1's word as i breathe in and out or out and in much would be done by me these monks are said to live earnestly keenly they develop mindfulness of death for the destruction of the us of us therefore monks train yourselves thus we will live earnestly keenly will we develop mindfulness of death for the destruction of the asavas train yourselves thus monks as the end of the sultan this mindfulness of death is a very important practice for us to in order that we may turn away from the world and practice the holy life if we were not to remind ourselves that this body is going to die 1 day and that 1 day is not very far away then we would be still be very interested in the world because there are many things in the world especially nowadays and that would attract us but it is only because we keep reminding ourselves that death would come in the not too distant time that we have this earnestness and this incentive to practice the holy life you notice the buddha said about development [Music] were i to abide mindful of the exalted ones were much would be done by me this mindful of the buddha's word that is mindfulness of the dhammala dhammanusati 1 of the objects of the satipatana meditation [Music] and in this uh suta we can see that this uh sati in the sati patanas some people translate it as contemplating the thoughts but here it would seem to be you know dhamma is the buddha's words contemplating the buddha's words because by contemplating the buddha's words and the discourses of the buddha we that is 1 way to become enlightened or become an arya as i mentioned before in 1 of the sutas in the chapter of the fives the buddha said there are 5 times a person can become an arya 1 is listening to dhamma another 1 is teaching dhamma another 1 is repeating dhamma another 1 is reflecting on dhamma and then the fifth 1 is during meditation so in this utta the buddha said now if we only reflect on death and in a way that you think you ought to reflect on the buddha's words only say like once a day or once a day and night then the buddha said that is being very slack that is not being earnest about the practice but 1 who develops mindfulness of death so that with every breath he reminds himself of death or with every mouthful of food he reminds himself of death and he thinks of the buddha's words then such a person is said to be earnest in other words the buddha is trying to tell us that as often as possible we should remember to practice the mindfulness of death as often as we can we should uh contemplate on the buddha's words the nexuta is
